mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean returns when FEDFUNDS increases, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over all 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.0%, labor market slack signals weakening aggregate demand and rising recession probability, causing investors to de-risk and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in greater than 65% of periods where UNRATE exceeds 5.0%, or the signal loses statistical significance with p-value above 0.10 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Strong retail sales growth combined with a declining federal funds rate indicates robust consumer spending supported by cheap credit, which drives corporate revenue growth and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to produce positive returns when RSAFS growth exceeds 2% and FEDFUNDS falls, or the interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and rising business investment, which signals near-term economic strength and triggers positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration fails to predict positive 5-day returns during recovery phases, or the predictive alpha decays to zero within 3 trading days.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls indicates a strengthening labor market and rising household income, which supports consumer spending and corporate earnings, yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows no correlation with 20-day forward returns, or Rank IC remains below 0.01 across all tested rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index surpasses 215.0, elevated inflation expectations force markets to price in aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity multiples and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL levels above 215.0 coincide with positive 60-day forward returns in greater than 70% of observations, or the threshold effect vanishes in out-of-sample validation.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in housing starts and industrial production signals synchronized growth across residential and manufacturing sectors, boosting aggregate demand and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields negative or flat returns when both series rise concurrently, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero in panel regressions.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly decline in real gross domestic product confirms economic contraction, triggering recession risk premiums and capital flight from equities, which drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Negative GDP growth fails to predict negative 60-day returns during contraction phases, or the signal predictive power is fully subsumed by lagged market momentum.
